message:
[P2] fcp-mesh: replace IbltPlaceholder JSON change log with production CBOR Iblt on gossip wire

Closes flywheel_connectors-kzrfp.

Gossip anti-entropy previously carried a JSON-serialized
`VecDeque<(ObjectId, Option<u32>)>` of recent local changes in
`summary.iblt` rather than an actual IBLT sketch. Under any divergence
larger than `reconciliation_batch_size` the placeholder silently
dropped the oldest changes from the front of the deque, so peers
stopped converging on the long tail. The wire format also violated
the NORMATIVE "precise set reconciliation" claim in the struct
docstring and was not CBOR-canonical.

Migration (in-place on the existing public type for ABI continuity):

* `IbltPlaceholder` is now a thin wrapper over the production `Iblt`
  from `crate::iblt`. Field shape is `{iblt: Iblt, cell_count: usize,
  change_seq: u64}` — the inherited `change_seq` metric is retained
  so existing observers continue to see monotonic counts.
* `new()` / `with_max_changes(n)` size the sketch via
  `Iblt::recommended_cell_count(n)` (floored at
  `MIN_RECOMMENDED_IBLT_CELLS = 64`); `with_max_changes(0)` still
  produces a valid minimum-sized sketch instead of a no-op deque.
* `note_local_change(object_id, esi)` now calls `iblt.insert(*object_id)`
  and drops `esi` (IBLT reconciles objects; ESI-level flow is handled
  by the parallel `symbol_filter` / `symbol_availability` paths on
  `GossipState`). Earlier placeholder mixed objects and (obj, esi)
  into the same change log, which was neither IBLT-compatible nor
  useful for object-level set reconciliation.
* `encode()` / `decode_with_limits()` serialize via canonical CBOR
  (`ciborium`), moved from dev-dependencies into regular
  dependencies. The empty-byte payload is the new fallback-marker
  value used by `MeshGossip::create_summary` when a sketch exceeds
  `max_iblt_bytes` (previously `b"[]"`).
* `IbltDecodeError::TooManyChanges` variant is retained for ABI
  continuity but now compares IBLT cell count against
  `Iblt::recommended_cell_count(max_changes)` — peers that ship a
  sketch declaring more cells than our budget are rejected.
* `recent_changes()` kept as a no-op accessor returning `Vec::new()`
  so the public surface stays source-compatible with the fuzz target
  and any external caller that inspected the pre-migration shape.
* `as_iblt()` added to give direct read access to the underlying
  sketch for diff / subtract / peel calls.

Byte budget: `GossipConfig::max_iblt_bytes` scales from 48 to 128
bytes per expected-difference unit (CBOR cells are ~70 B each vs.
JSON tuples' ~40 B), with the floor raised from 512 to 8192 bytes to
cover the minimum 64-cell sketch. The `GossipSummary.iblt` wire field
now carries CBOR-encoded `Iblt`, not JSON.

New regression test
`iblt_reconciliation_recovers_full_divergence` demonstrates the
underlying bug: after 64 shared inserts plus 40 local-only inserts
beyond any reasonable `reconciliation_batch_size` window, a peer
decode-and-subtract recovers exactly the 40 local-only object IDs
via IBLT peel. The pre-migration deque placeholder would have
truncated the oldest shared changes and silently stopped converging.

Test updates (public API shape preserved, assertions migrated):
* `iblt_tracks_changes` / `iblt_change_seq_increments_regardless_of_budget`
  — drop `recent_changes().len()` assertions, keep `change_seq()`.
* `iblt_encode_is_cbor_iblt_roundtrip` — verify encode output parses
  as a real `Iblt` and peels to the inserted set.
* `iblt_encode_decode_roundtrip` — uses two distinct object IDs
  (IBLT peel requires ±1 pure cells, so inserting the same ID twice
  produces an unpeelable count=2 cell).
* `iblt_decode_rejects_oversized_cell_count` — replaces the old
  change-count rejection with a cell-count rejection against a sketch
  that declared a larger budget than the peer accepts.
* `iblt_decode_empty_bytes_returns_empty` / `iblt_clear_and_encode`
  / `iblt_clear` — assert emptiness via self-subtract-and-peel rather
  than by inspecting the old change log.
* `iblt_default` / `iblt_entry_count` — `entry_count()` now reports
  IBLT cell count, which is non-zero even for an empty sketch.
* `mesh_gossip_create_summary_stays_within_iblt_budget` — replaces
  the legacy fallback-on-announce-flood test. Production sketch size
  is derived from batch size (not announce volume), so the fallback
  path is now only reachable from pathological external wire growth;
  the new test asserts the sketch is non-empty, within budget, and
  decodable by a peer with matching batch size.
* `handle_summary_caps_peer_state_cardinality` — switches the
  synthetic summary payload from `b"[]"` to `Vec::new()` (the new
  fallback marker).
* `gossip_config_max_iblt_bytes_*` — updated to match the 128 B /
  unit factor and the 8192 B floor.
* `mesh_post_verify_summary` fuzz target — mode 0 now emits an empty
  payload (accepted) and mode 1 emits `b"not-cbor"` (rejected with
  `InvalidEncoding`) so the "oversized" and "malformed" branches
  still exercise the post-verify handler.

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t5 cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib gossip::
    → 135 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 1044 filtered out.

Two unrelated `coordinator::` failures observed in the full
`cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib` run
(`acquire_does_not_panic_on_u64_max_fencing_token_with_expired_lease`
and `acquire_rebases_fencing_token_above_observed_history`) are
outside this change's scope and live in another pane's domain — the
file was last touched by commits `8a0d4959` / `1de6f11e` which are
not part of this diff.

Docs note (docs/FCP3_Pre_Cutover_Baseline.md §IbltPlaceholder) is
left to a follow-up; the type comment is updated in place at the
struct definition to describe the new production backing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,6 +27,7 @@ fcp-tailscale = { path = "../fcp-tailscale" }
 hex.workspace = true
 hkdf = "0.12"
 hmac = "0.12"
+ciborium.workspace = true
 rand.workspace = true
 serde.workspace = true
 serde_json.workspace = true
@@ -37,7 +38,6 @@ xorf.workspace = true
 zeroize.workspace = true
 
 [dev-dependencies]
-ciborium.workspace = true
 criterion.workspace = true
 fcp-async-core = { path = "../fcp-async-core" }
 fcp-cbor = { path = "../fcp-cbor" }

@@ -49,8 +49,13 @@ fn test_node(config: GossipConfig) -> MeshNode {
 
 fn summary_iblt(mode: u8, raw: &[u8], max_iblt_bytes: usize) -> Vec<u8> {
     match mode % 4 {
-        0 => b"[]".to_vec(),
-        1 => b"not-json".to_vec(),
+        // Empty payload: decode_with_limits routes to the "empty
+        // sketch sized for batch" branch (matches create_summary's
+        // over-budget fallback marker).
+        0 => Vec::new(),
+        // Malformed CBOR: decode_with_limits rejects with InvalidEncoding.
+        1 => b"not-cbor".to_vec(),
+        // Oversized payload: rejected before parse.
         2 => vec![0u8; max_iblt_bytes.saturating_add(1)],
         _ => raw
             .iter()
